trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Johannes Voss, a Leipzig-based artist who has illustrated more than 150 pieces for Magic: The Gathering since he began working with the game in 2010. Hipsters of the Coast describes him as 'well known for his cornucopia of color and dramatic compositions.' His collector-market profile is significant: two playmat Kickstarters with Original Magic Art both sold out, with multiple exclusive designs. Tome Anima shares a set with another Voss piece, Sanctum of All (also M21), demonstrating his volume within a single set. [Hipsters of the Coast: 'Johannes Voss and Original Magic Art Launch New Playmat Kickstarter for 2023', hipstersofthecoast.com, 2023]
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Johannes Voss and Original Magic Art Launch New Playmat Kickstarter for 2023, 2023

trivia_bullets.text: Tome Anima is a 3/3 Spirit for {3}{U} with a conditional evasion ability: it can't be blocked as long as you've drawn two or more cards this turn. This connects it directly to blue's card-draw engine — the creature rewards proactive draw-spell sequencing with guaranteed damage. EDHREC rank: 26,276 — one of the lowest adoption rates in the corpus, consistent with its role as a core-set Limited filler rather than a Commander or Pauper staple. Legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander. [Scryfall: edhrec_rank=26276, legalities]

"Sometimes knowledge takes on a life of its own." — no speaker attributed. [Scryfall] A tight double-meaning: literal (the Spirit is animated knowledge from a library) and figurative (ideas becoming autonomous forces). Sits well alongside the Vision's read of the art as 'the moment a piece of accumulated knowledge becomes self-aware.'

Tome Anima's Spirit creature type and library setting situate it in M21's blue-learning subtheme, which runs alongside the set's planeswalker-companion suite. M21's design philosophy emphasized accessible keyword mechanics — Tome Anima's evasion condition (draw 2+ cards) is simple enough for new players to track while rewarding spell-sequencing skill. [Scryfall: set=m21, released_at=2020-07-03] [Wizards: Odds & Ends Core Set 2021, 2020-06-29]
